records.title: How to Replace Alternator 2009-2010 Ford F-150 5.4L V8
records.problemAddressed: Charging failure, battery drain, or alternator replacement on the 5.4 truck.
procedureSummary: Relieve belt tension and remove the belt from the alternator pulley.
Remove the alternator fasteners and electrical connections.
Install the replacement alternator and reassemble.
warnings: Disconnect battery before working on the charging system.
Confirm output rating and pulley configuration before ordering.

partsUsed.notes: 150A, 12VDC alternator shown on the guide page.

records.title: How to Replace Rear Wheel Seals 2009-2011 Ford F-150
records.problemAddressed: Rear axle seal leak, bearing/seal service on 2009-2011 F-150 trucks.
procedureSummary: Raise and support the truck, then remove the wheel and brake hardware.
Drain the axle as needed and remove the axle shaft.
Drive out the old seal and bearing, then install the replacements.
Reassemble, refill gear oil, and verify leak-free operation.
warnings: Keep gear oil and brake friction surfaces separated.
Rear axle work can expand quickly if bearings or shafts are already damaged.

partsUsed.notes: Rear wheel seal listed on the guide page.
Rear wheel bearing and seal kit listed on the guide page.

records.title: How to Replace Headlight Bulbs 2009-2014 Ford F 150
records.problemAddressed: Headlight bulb replacement and front lamp access.
procedureSummary: Open access to the headlamp housing.
Remove the bulb retainer and disconnect the old bulb.
Install the new bulb without contaminating the glass.
Recheck beam operation before closing up.
warnings: Do not touch the bulb glass with bare hands.
Inspect the socket and connector if the bulb failed early or the housing shows heat damage.

partsUsed.notes: Guide covers replacement bulbs rather than a housing assembly.

records.title: How to Replace Outer Tie Rod Ends 2015-2020 Ford F-150
records.problemAddressed: Outer tie rod replacement; fitment list on the guide includes 2010-2014 F-150 trucks.
procedureSummary: Loosen the outer tie rod end and count turns to preserve approximate alignment.
Separate the tie rod end from the knuckle.
Install the replacement and torque to spec.
warnings: Any steering linkage repair needs a follow-up alignment.
Rusty suspension hardware can turn a simple tie rod job into a cut-off job.

partsUsed.notes: Guide page centers on TRQ outer tie rods.
